new_classes.label_en: Wine with Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) in containers > 2 L (subheading 2204 29 79)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter geografischer Angabe (g.g.A.) in Behältnissen > 2 L (Unterposition 2204 29 79)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) within the meaning of additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2204 29 79.

coverage_explanation: The three primary distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) PGI quality (via ProtectedGeographicalIndicationQuality restriction), (2) container volume > 2 L (via LargeContainer part restriction), and (3) non-semi-sparkling wine character (complement of SemiSparklingPressureQuality, distinguishing from 2204 29 10). The legal note additionally references additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, which defines the precise legal scope of 'PGI' for wine purposes. This chapter-level additional note individual is not present in the TBox, so the precise definitional cross-reference cannot be axiomatised directly. Furthermore, the sibling code 22042980 shares the same note and the boundary between the two codes (likely based on actual alcoholic strength or variety) is not specified in the provided legal text, preventing full discrimination between siblings at this level. Coverage is therefore estimated at 0.75.
